jokes.text: Everything look like a mexican soap opera.
jokes.setup: Character commenting on how things look weird
jokes.punchline: Comparing the appearance to a Mexican soap opera
jokes.explanation: Simple observational humor with decent visual comparison but fairly predictable

jokes.text: God, I can see every line and pore on your face!
jokes.setup: Response to question about things looking weird
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest assessment of someone's face
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor with excellent economy - gets to the uncomfortable truth quickly

jokes.text: You look like a YMCA climbing wall.
jokes.setup: Following up on the brutal face assessment
jokes.punchline: Specific, absurd visual comparison to a climbing wall
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with a hilariously specific and unexpected visual comparison. Very quotable.

jokes.text: What is that word? Brains.
jokes.setup: Character struggling to remember a word about face cream destroying lab rats
jokes.punchline: The word they're looking for is 'brains'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing someone's ditzy nature with dark humor about brain-destroying cosmetics

jokes.text: Remember what happened that time I tried to give up refined sugars?
jokes.setup: Jack saying giving up drinking might not be good for him
jokes.punchline: Reference to a past incident with giving up sugar
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - implies Jack had a dramatic reaction to dietary changes

jokes.text: Looking good, Liz.
jokes.setup: After extensive discussion of how terrible Liz looks
jokes.punchline: Completely contradictory compliment
jokes.explanation: Simple ironic reversal, effective timing

jokes.text: Our bodies, we don't want all that processed junk. I don't know if you've read Michael Pollan... Who wants donuts? I'll kill you!
jokes.setup: Liz talking about healthy eating and Michael Pollan
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction when offered donuts, violent reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz Lemon character moment - hypocritical about health, violent food obsession. Great structure.

jokes.text: How come you better-looking in your memory? My memory has Seinfeld money.
jokes.setup: Question about looking better in memory
jokes.punchline: Comparing memory enhancement to Seinfeld's wealth
jokes.explanation: Clever metaphor connecting memory enhancement to wealth/production values

jokes.text: And therefore, Avery has got me this very nice tea set, some knitting needles and yarn and a book on sleight-of-hand magic.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining how he's replacing his drinking ritual
jokes.punchline: The bizarre combination of genteel and random replacement activities
jokes.explanation: Good character humor - Jack's sophisticated approach to replacing drinking with incongruous activities

jokes.text: That's as far as I've gotten.
jokes.setup: After listing all his new hobbies
jokes.punchline: Admitting he's made no progress
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan admission of failure

jokes.text: I'm dreading watching it sober.
jokes.setup: Jack saying he'll go watch the show
jokes.punchline: Admitting he needs to be drunk to enjoy the show he produces
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark humor about the quality of their own show, delivered with Jack's characteristic honesty

jokes.text: I seem to have forgotten to put you on it.
jokes.setup: Liz asking if Jack knows it's her birthday, assistant checking birthday list
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of forgetting to include Liz
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Liz's birthday disappointment, delivered with bureaucratic indifference

jokes.text: Yeah, hurry up, Aladdin. Before Jasmine is forced to marry Jafar.
jokes.setup: Someone asking Jonathan for a pen
jokes.punchline: Extending the Aladdin metaphor with story urgency
jokes.explanation: Decent extension of the Aladdin joke with narrative urgency

jokes.text: Are you turning 1,000?
jokes.setup: Liz saying it's a pretty big birthday
jokes.punchline: Absurdly exaggerated age guess
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation joke about Liz's age with good economy

jokes.text: Really, you want to play this game with a comedy writer?
jokes.setup: Exchange about Liz's age
jokes.punchline: Liz asserting her professional advantage in comedy
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor and character assertion of professional skills

jokes.text: Yes, bullet in brain move. Much hospital.
jokes.setup: Liz saying she hasn't seen Jadwiga in a long time
jokes.punchline: Jadwiga's broken English explanation of being shot in the head
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered in broken English, casual mention of traumatic brain injury

jokes.text: Happy days is my favorite show.
jokes.setup: Liz trying to explain about her birthday party
jokes.punchline: Jadwiga completely misunderstanding, talking about TV show
jokes.explanation: Good miscommunication humor showing Jadwiga's language barrier and random TV preference

jokes.text: His office is bathroom, just like Jadwiga.
jokes.setup: Talking about liking Fonzi
jokes.punchline: Jadwiga comparing her workplace to Fonzi's bathroom office
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Jadwiga's working conditions with pop culture reference

jokes.text: A Mr. Brett Favré stopped by, and dropped off this picture of a hot dog.
jokes.setup: Assistant delivering messages
jokes.punchline: Brett Favre's bizarre message delivery method
jokes.explanation: Absurdist celebrity cameo reference with bizarre communication method

jokes.text: I guess they're geniuses for getting stuck in a mine.
jokes.setup: Chilean miners being angry about something Tracy said
jokes.punchline: Tracy's sarcastic dismissal of their intelligence
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy character moment - inappropriate response to serious situation

jokes.text: Thanks, Obama care.
jokes.setup: Being told he can't get a prescription for ecstasy
jokes.punchline: Blaming Obamacare for not being able to get illegal drugs
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic from Tracy, blaming healthcare policy for drug prohibition

jokes.text: Of course, not. His album doesn't drop until December.
jokes.setup: Liz saying Tracy's lizard can't be the musical guest
jokes.punchline: Tracy treating his pet lizard as a recording artist with an album
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist escalation - treating a pet lizard as a serious recording artist

jokes.text: It was even funnier than the porn version
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining he watched the Carol Burnett Show
jokes.punchline: Implying there's a porn version he's seen
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy character humor - casually mentioning inappropriate content

jokes.text: You should have. Those dudes were awesome.
jokes.setup: Reference to Tracy wanting to hire two strippers
jokes.punchline: Revealing the strippers were men
jokes.explanation: Good character moment revealing Tracy's unexpected preferences

jokes.text: I swear on my mother's grape. - Did you say grave or grape? - Yes, good-bye.
jokes.setup: Tracy making a promise and mispronouncing
jokes.punchline: Ambiguous answer to clarification question
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay with ambiguous response that could mean either word

jokes.text: It was Angels in America, Tray!
jokes.setup: Tracy saying he laughed hard at Dotcom's play
jokes.punchline: Dotcom revealing it was the serious AIDS drama Angels in America
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe humor - Tracy laughing at a serious AIDS drama

jokes.text: Whoever standing next to him, it's stupid! That guy is stupid. Now, she is. Now they are. Now I were.
jokes.setup: Assistant explaining Lutz's t-shirt that makes people look stupid
jokes.punchline: Grammar deteriorating as the shirt affects the speaker
jokes.explanation: Clever visual gag concept with escalating grammatical deterioration showing the effect in real time

jokes.text: Wait, did I just hear that correctly? Because last year, I wrote a song called It's your Birthday, Slut.
jokes.setup: Mention of song 'It's your B-day, Bitch'
jokes.punchline: Liz revealing she wrote a similar but more offensive version
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Liz's crude songwriting and competitive nature

jokes.text: You say that it's your birthday Time to skank it up hard Choke a cup with your panties
jokes.setup: Liz singing her birthday song
jokes.punchline: Increasingly crude and nonsensical lyrics
jokes.explanation: Cringe humor showing Liz's terrible songwriting abilities with inappropriate lyrics

jokes.text: Did you just knit that?
jokes.setup: Jack has taken up knitting as a drinking replacement
jokes.punchline: Jack has apparently knitted something during the conversation
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Jack's new hobbies with absurd speed of completion

jokes.text: Get out of here!
jokes.setup: Jack trying to be entertained to distract from drinking
jokes.punchline: Jack's dramatic rejection of Liz's theatrical performance
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing Jack's standards even while desperate for distraction

jokes.text: Fox News. A division of Fox nonsense incorporated.
jokes.setup: Fake Fox News segment
jokes.punchline: Deliberately obvious corporate structure joke
jokes.explanation: Standard media satire with obvious corporate criticism

jokes.text: I'm doing something called 'breaking'!
jokes.setup: Tracy deliberately breaking character on air
jokes.punchline: Tracy announcing his deliberate rule-breaking
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor and character consistency - Tracy doing exactly what he said he'd do

jokes.text: But couldn't the real cause of E.D. be that we haven't produced a good doing-it song since Close the Door by Teddy Pendergrass?
jokes.setup: Medical explanation for erectile dysfunction
jokes.punchline: Blaming lack of good sex music
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dr. Spaceman moment - completely ridiculous medical theory delivered with authority

jokes.text: Tie me to my radiator and put food just out of reach
jokes.setup: Dr. Spaceman singing romantic lyrics
jokes.punchline: Lyrics taking a disturbing turn toward captivity
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor escalation - romantic song becoming disturbing captivity scenario

jokes.text: I lookout for her, we're friends with benefits.
jokes.setup: Pete talking about looking out for Jadwiga
jokes.punchline: Using 'friends with benefits' in completely wrong context
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection using sexual terminology in innocent context, perfect for Pete's character

jokes.text: They're rich and they have attention deficit disorder.
jokes.setup: Question about why anybody does anything
jokes.punchline: Pete's cynical assessment of human motivation
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about modern society with Pete's characteristic cynicism

jokes.text: Did you say scout or krout?
jokes.setup: Tracy saying 'Krout's honor'
jokes.punchline: Liz questioning the pronunciation, echoing earlier grape/grave joke
jokes.explanation: Decent callback to earlier pronunciation joke pattern

jokes.text: The in-flight meal was a frittata. Oh, my God. At night?
jokes.setup: Carol describing his terrible flight
jokes.punchline: Liz's horror at the inappropriateness of serving frittata at night
jokes.explanation: Good food-related humor showing Liz's priorities and knowledge of meal timing

jokes.text: I also need you to TiVO Bones for me in case I survive.
jokes.setup: Carol asking Liz to clear out his porn if he dies
jokes.punchline: Adding mundane TV recording request as afterthought to death preparations
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between life-or-death situation and mundane TV preferences

jokes.text: I was on stage in Pippin with Irene Ryan when she died, and I kept on going.
jokes.setup: Jenna claiming she never breaks during performance
jokes.punchline: Claiming she continued performing while her co-star literally died
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor showing Jenna's extreme commitment to performance over human decency

jokes.text: Sit on it.
jokes.setup: Liz greeting Jadwiga again
jokes.punchline: Jadwiga using Fonzie's catchphrase inappropriately
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Jadwiga's Fonzie obsession with perfect economy

jokes.text: It gives these ability to hit on women and later when we're married to tune them out.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining why men need alcohol
jokes.punchline: Cynical view of alcohol's role in relationships
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about marriage and gender relations in Jack's characteristic style

jokes.text: Damn you, she-beasts!
jokes.setup: Jack's frustration building
jokes.punchline: Jack's dramatic outburst blaming women
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Jack's breakdown with characteristic misogyny

jokes.text: Yes, but my penis was smaller.
jokes.setup: Acknowledging he was fitter in the past
jokes.punchline: Bizarre non-sequitur about penis size
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist non-sequitur with perfect economy and quotability

jokes.text: I could really drink a forty right now.
jokes.setup: Liz talking about turning 40
jokes.punchline: Connecting her age to 40-ounce beer bottles
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay connecting age milestone to alcohol preference

jokes.text: My Oprah wig is falling off!
jokes.setup: Live show mishaps happening
jokes.punchline: Jenna's wig malfunction during Oprah impression
jokes.explanation: Standard live TV mishap humor with visual component

jokes.text: It is nipple time!
jokes.setup: Jenna threatening a wardrobe malfunction
jokes.punchline: Jenna's dramatic announcement of her planned exposure
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and callback to Jenna's threat with memorable phrasing

jokes.text: Number of which became available after a giant explosion at a Josh Groban's concert.
jokes.setup: Dr. Drew explaining he has a female hand transplant
jokes.punchline: Bizarre explanation involving Josh Groban concert disaster
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark absurdist humor connecting celebrity to tragedy in unexpected way

jokes.text: Does it mean that what happened can be used to power a lumbermill?
jokes.setup: Jonathan saying they don't have expression 'water under the bridge' in Canada
jokes.punchline: Jonathan's literal interpretation involving lumber mills
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist character humor with creative literal interpretation of idiom

jokes.text: The capital one venture card is amazing. They give double miles every day for every purchase!
jokes.setup: Pete suggesting cutting product placement
jokes.punchline: Pete accidentally doing product placement while talking about cutting it
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor about product placement with perfect ironic execution

jokes.text: You stole an old cleaning lady's birthday just to make me happy? In my defense, yes.
jokes.setup: Liz realizing they stole Jadwiga's party
jokes.punchline: Jack's unapologetic confirmation
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Jack's unapologetic selfishness with perfect economy

jokes.text: You may turn me into a crow.
jokes.setup: Jack saying it's dangerous to deny wishes of an old spinster
jokes.punchline: Jack's fear of being transformed by Liz's supposed witch powers
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor showing Jack's superstitious side with fairy tale reference

jokes.text: You're halfway to death.
jokes.setup: Jack toasting to Liz Lemon
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest assessment of her age milestone
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor toast with perfect Jack delivery and maximum economy
